rationale: Use the Transparent California `POLICE` candidate pool but keep only rows whose job title actually contains a police title to remove false positives caused by name matches.
Library titles remain in the actual-pay scrape because the 2024 results are still small enough to capture completely and provide an academic-support worker benchmark.
The UC president is the clearest top-executive comparison point for worker-pay analysis.
The `CHAN` query is broad, so the analytic cohort keeps only exact chancellor titles to avoid pulling in assistant-to-chancellor and vice-chancellor rows.
